title: Agent Audit — Vanta vs Hermes vs Goose (+ Claude Code)
status: complete — 4-way deep-dive: code, docs, issues, local-Hermes delta, ND-vision filter

vision.statement: Vanta is a FULL-CAPABILITY personal operator — it builds, ships, researches, runs companies, handles comms and calendar: everything Hermes does. The difference is the DESIGN LENS. Vanta is built executive-function-first: it supplies the initiation, sequencing, follow-through, working memory, and calm that every other agent assumes the human already brings. This is inclusive design, NOT accessibility-narrowing — the curb-cut effect: build for the people for whom finishing is hardest (executive-function disorders, ADHD, autism, dyslexia, dyscalculia, aphantasia — and anyone under stress, overload, or fatigue) and you build a better operator for EVERYONE. Capability is table stakes; the moat is follow-through — the first operator that helps you FINISH, not just do more. Grounded in Vanta's neurodivergent-first brain identity and the operator's own documented pattern (ideas-rich, fast to build, hard to finish, prone to bouncing and over-scoping). KEY FINDING: Hermes treats accessibility/ND as edge cases and Goose ignores it; BOTH assume the user supplies executive function. Vanta's wedge is to supply it.
thesis: Capability is table stakes. Vanta matches Hermes's breadth (build / ship / run companies / research / comms). The differentiator is what happens to the HUMAN's executive function — not the model's capability.

Every agent today assumes the human supplies initiation, sequencing, and follow-through. Nobody has perfect executive function; ND people just hit that wall first and hardest — which is what makes the design failure visible.

Inclusive, not merely accessible — the curb-cut effect. Solve for the margin (executive-function disorders) and the result is better for everyone: under stress, overload, or fatigue, every brain becomes the edge case. Accessibility narrows; inclusivity is the real lens, and it aims at everyone.

The moat is follow-through: hold the thread, park the tangent, name the next step, get to DONE. Proof-of-concept already exists — Vanta's own anti-drift CLAUDE.md (PARKED.md, 'define Done', ship-ugly-first, one-feature-end-to-end, 'while-we're-at-it banned') is an executive-function prosthesis bolted onto a general agent. Make it native and universal.

Not a narrowing of scope — a sharpening of WHY. Vanta does everything a top operator does; it is simply the only one designed so that a brain that floods with ideas and stalls before the finish line still ships.

filters.name: Initiation friction
filters.desc: Reduces task activation cost — lowers the 'getting started' barrier (the core ADHD/exec-function wall).

filters.name: Externalized memory
filters.desc: Offloads working memory — remembers goals, context, where you left off, what was decided.

filters.name: Calm output
filters.desc: Low-arousal, low-firehose, sensory-regulating output — never a wall of noise.

filters.name: Low decision load
filters.desc: Sensible defaults; doesn't demand constant micro-choices (decision fatigue / analysis paralysis).

filters.name: Structure & follow-through
filters.desc: Breaks down, sequences, nudges, tracks — supports planning and follow-through deficits.

filters.name: Reliability / trust
filters.desc: Predictable, safe, honest — removes the vigilance burden. Safe-by-construction = ND feature.

vision.curationBias: RUTHLESS by design — but DROP means 'adds cognitive load / decision surface', NOT 'reduces capability'. Vanta keeps full operator breadth (build/ship/run/research/comms); what it drops is the noise (recipe marketplaces, MoA ensembles, integration sprawl, IDE adapters) that demands choices and attention without helping you finish. The audit's job is a SMALL curated pull-set + a LOT of explicit DROP. Default verdict is DROP unless a feature either (a) supplies executive function (≥2 EF filters, low cognitive cost) or (b) is core operator capability Vanta lacks. Note the live irony: six escalating 'add to goal' messages this session IS the bounce/over-scope pattern — exactly the thing Vanta should help contain. Result: ~8 worthy pulls, ~16 explicit drops. Goose: ~80% of distinct features DROP (dev-throughput). Hermes: capability kept, breadth-noise dropped, the EF signals it treats as edge cases become Vanta's spine.

agents.name: Vanta
agents.tagline: Trusted operator: knows the goal, enforces scope on every action, reports only verified output.

agents.name: Hermes (upstream)
agents.lang: Python (~25k tests, 70+ tools, 28 toolsets, 20 platform adapters)
agents.tagline: The self-improving AI agent — built-in learning loop, skills from experience, $5 VPS, text it from Telegram.
agents.vsVanta: Vanta's parent. Vanta hardens Hermes's breadth into a kernel-enforced safety contract + ND spine. Hermes maximizes capability for builders; Vanta narrows to ND executive-function.

agents.name: Hermes (local customized)
agents.tagline: Jason's customized Hermes — the bar Vanta set out to match then exceed.
agents.vsVanta: Customizations: gateway streaming dispatch (now upstreamed), ai-gateway provider plugin, browser_providers, telegram/cron tweaks.

agents.name: Goose
agents.tagline: Native open-source AI agent — desktop, CLI, API — for code & workflows.
agents.vsVanta: Different lineage (Block). Dev-team-throughput agent. ~80% of distinct features DROP for an ND assistant; two ideas worth pulling (top-of-mind reminders, interactive plan mode).

agents.name: Claude Code (reference)
agents.tagline: The reference harness — surveyed for plumbing/permission/runnability patterns.
agents.vsVanta: Study-only; reimplement clean. Findings are infra-level (permission protocol, per-model capability matrix, activity-feed extraction).

vantaInventory.note: Vanta's current surface — the dedupe baseline. 675 tests green (648 TS + 27 Rust).

categories.name: Architecture
items: Rust safety kernel (assess() gates every tool call: Allow/Ask/Block) — deterministic, FAIL-CLOSED
Approvals queue + goal ledger + event log
TS agent layer (Node22/ESM/tsx, no build step)
Kernel is the boundary — TS cannot bypass it

categories.name: TUI / UX
items: React/Ink streaming transcript + tool activity + spinner
Inline approvals, slash commands, custom composer
TUI-SMOOTH: clean per-tool activity feed (no JSON/temp-path firehose)

categories.name: Prompt
items: Skill index injected; recall loads full body

categories.name: Self-improvement
items: Background review → writes skills tagged vanta-learned
Safe curator (archives only stale learned skills, never deletes)

categories.name: Autonomy / reach
items: MCP: consume (mount servers) + serve (Vanta as MCP server)
Cron schedule, project rooms, operator modes, model routing

ndVisionAlignment.note: How Vanta's EXISTING features already serve the ND vision — and where the gaps are.

strong.why: Removes vigilance burden — the operator never polices the agent; the boundary is physical. Stronger than Goose's fail-open LLM reviewer or Hermes's in-process approval.
Externalizes goals and context across sessions — offloads working memory; supports follow-through.
Builds a persistent model of the user; the ND framing is already encoded.
Just shipped — kills the firehose; low-arousal output. Vanta LEADS all three on calm output.

gaps.gap: No explicit task-initiation / 'just start the next tiny step' affordance
gaps.note: PULL #1 — the activation wall is the central ND exec-function need; NONE of the 3 agents target it directly. Vanta's differentiator.
PULL — Hermes 'converse mode' + Goose 'plan mode' both exist; Vanta's autonomy L1 is adjacent but not an interactive front-door.

gaps.gap: No top-of-mind / pinned-reminder injected every turn
No proactive nudge cadence (gentle check-ins / standups)
gaps.note: PULL — Hermes users built ADHD standups + unprompted reminders on top; make it native.
PULL — Hermes shadow-git checkpoints; reduces anxiety around destructive ops.
Candidate — broad ND, not just exec function.

operatorPatterns.note: The vision grounded in the operator's REAL, documented executive-function patterns (from ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md, the portfolio state, this repo's PARKED.md, and this very session). Each pattern → the Vanta feature that supplies the missing executive function. These are universal: under stress/overload, every operator hits them — the curb-cut effect in action. Vanta running this scan on itself ('show me my patterns') is itself an ND-support feature.

items.pattern: Idea flood — great ideas, constantly bouncing to the next one
items.evidence: CLAUDE.md 'PARKED.md for mid-build ideas. Don't build them. Don't debate them. Park.'; this repo has 6 parked items; this session: 6 escalating 'add to goal' messages.
items.feature: PARKED-style tangent capture + ND4 top-of-mind reminder (pins the ONE active goal) + ND2/ND3 clarify/plan-first as a scope gate

items.pattern: Fast to start & build, can't FINISH
items.evidence: CLAUDE.md 'Jason over-engineers and doesn't ship'; portfolio: gripe stalled ~3 weeks, lint scaffolded.
items.feature: ND1 task-initiation→completion (next tiny step toward an explicit DONE) + goal ledger tracks to completion + 'define Done in one sentence' as a ritual

items.pattern: Trouble figuring out what to do next (blank-page / next-step paralysis)
items.evidence: stated by the operator; classic EF working-memory + initiation deficit.
items.feature: ND1 surfaces ONE concrete next step; ND3 plan-first sequences the whole thing before acting

items.evidence: CLAUDE.md '"While we're at it" banned'; 'Push back on Jason's own scope creep. Name it.'
items.feature: ND3 plan-first confirm-gate + clarify tool; scope-creep naming promoted from a behavior to a product feature

items.pattern: Loses the thread / context across sessions
items.feature: F2 stack: goal ledger, per-goal memory, ND4 top-of-mind, U2 @-context references

items.pattern: Sensory/attention overwhelm from noisy tool output
items.evidence: the firehose problem that drove TUI-SMOOTH this session.
items.feature: F3 stack: TUI-SMOOTH calm feed + UX-STREAM typed events (calm output on every surface)

items.pattern: Vigilance fatigue — having to police what the agent does
items.evidence: CLAUDE.md secrets/destructive-command guardrails; the whole point of the kernel.
items.feature: F6 stack: fail-closed kernel (safe by construction) + REL2 checkpoints/rollback (undo net)

comparison.note: Decision-level matrix. Cells: ✓ has · ~ partial · ✗ lacks. Verdict from the EF/ND filter. Refined by code+docs+issues deep-dive.

rows.note: Vanta's structural moat — safe by construction. Goose adversary-mode fails OPEN (reviewer error → call allowed).
Core ND differentiator; reinforced by Hermes's stream_events/dispatch 'eat noisy chrome per surface' pattern (PULL the concept).
Vanta inherited + hardened Hermes's loop; added autonomous build factory.
Low-friction capture = initiation aid. Vanta's 1 adapter is enough for one user; breadth is DROP.
Two independent agents converged here; strong ND fit (kills wrong-guess thrash + blank-page paralysis).
Hermes users hacked this for ADHD; make it native + gentle.
Cheap externalize-context affordance; aligns with Vanta's planned @-file mentions.
Prevents 400s on free-typed OpenRouter/Ollama models → reliability.
Explicit accessibility demand in Hermes user-stories (motor needs, blind user). ND-aligned.
Goose deepest, but Vanta's consume+serve covers the one-user need.
All three handle it; Hermes most pluggable.
Power-user infra breadth; Vanta's kernel already gates.

pulls.note: WORTH ADDING — curated, ND-aligned. The top tier (HIGH) become roadmap items; DEFER items are horizon. Small by design.

items.feature: Task-initiation affordance ('start the next tiny step')
items.why: The activation wall is the central ND exec-function need; NO agent surveyed targets it. Vanta's true differentiator — not a copy.

items.feature: clarify tool (ask one structured question before acting)
items.why: Kills wrong-guess thrash; ask-don't-assume = predictable + low-vigilance. Tiny surface, high fit.

items.feature: Plan-first / converse mode (clarify→steps→confirm→act)
items.why: Two agents converged. Opt-in act-gate that breaks blank-page paralysis; extends Vanta's autonomy L1 into an interactive front door.

items.feature: Top-of-mind reminder (pinned text injected every turn)
items.why: Smallest, highest-fit working-memory offload — 'what am I doing / don't forget X' always in context.

items.feature: Per-model capability matrix in providers/catalog
items.why: Free-typed OpenRouter/Ollama models 400 on unsupported thinking/effort params. Small declarative map fixes a real reliability gap (echoed by Goose issue #9529).

items.feature: Proactive nudge cadence (gentle native check-ins / standup)
items.why: Hermes users hacked this for ADHD on top of cron; make it native + gentle (not nagging — respect F3).

items.feature: Checkpoints + /rollback (shadow-git undo net)
items.why: Undo safety net = low-anxiety trust around destructive ops. Vanta's kernel blocks but doesn't snapshot/restore.

items.feature: Context references @file/@diff/@url (composer)
items.why: Cheap externalize-context affordance; less typing/recall. Already adjacent to Vanta's planned U2 @-file mentions — merge them.

items.feature: Typed stream-event contract + per-surface chrome suppression
items.why: Clean agent→surface event vocabulary; surfaces that can't render tool chrome 'eat' it. Reinforces TUI-SMOOTH (calm output). Port the design, not the Python.

items.feature: Voice conversational loop (live mic-in / spoken reply)
items.why: DEFER. Explicit accessibility demand (motor needs, blind user w/ NVDA). Vanta has speak/transcribe tools; the live loop is the gap.

items.feature: Recovery / degraded readline CLI (behind a flag)
items.why: DEFER. Resilience when Ink can't render (CI/headless/native-dep failure).

items.feature: Visible token/cost indicator (calm at-a-glance)
items.why: DEFER. A small calm dot, not a dashboard — borders on noise; keep minimal if pulled.

drops.note: LOSE TOTALLY / DO NOT BUILD — fails the ND filter, duplicates Vanta, or is platform-thinking-before-users.

items.feature: Adversary mode (LLM reviewer gates each tool call)
items.why: WEAKER duplicate of Vanta's kernel: LLM-judged + FAIL-OPEN (reviewer error → call allowed) vs Vanta's deterministic fail-closed assess(). Importing it would weaken the moat.

items.feature: Recipes + deeplinks + cookbook (shareable workflow packages)
items.why: Sharing ecosystem / marketplace = platform-thinking before users; adds choice load (F4 violation).

items.feature: Goose Apps / MCP-UI (embedded HTML mini-apps in chat)
items.why: Adds visual surface + cognitive load; plugin-UI platform-thinking. Vanta TUI stays calm.

items.feature: Code Mode (LLM writes JS in Deno runtime to chain tools)
items.why: Pure context/perf optimization for 5+ extensions; new runtime dep. Dev-power-tooling, anti-fit.

items.feature: ML prompt-injection scanner (external HF endpoint)
items.why: Requires external service/token; enterprise noise for a solo ND user.

items.feature: Kanban multi-profile board
items.why: Multi-agent fleet coordination = platform-thinking. Vanta's todo + delegate + swarm cover the single-user need.

items.feature: Mixture-of-Agents (MoA) ensemble tool
items.why: Quality-via-ensemble dev tooling; adds cost/latency/noise, no EF benefit.

items.feature: ACP / IDE adapter (VS Code/Zed/JetBrains)
items.why: IDE-integration breadth; Vanta is a standalone operator, not an IDE plugin.

items.feature: 6 terminal backends (docker/ssh/modal/daytona/singularity)
items.why: Power-user infra breadth; Vanta's Rust kernel already gates execution.

items.feature: Integration sprawl (X/Spotify/HomeAssistant/MS-Graph/image+video gen)
items.why: Breadth that adds cognitive load + config burden; not an EF need.

items.feature: China/regional platform adapters (QQ, Feishu, WeCom) + i18n desktop
items.why: Platform/locale breadth; Vanta is single-user, English, macOS-first.

items.feature: ai-gateway model-provider plugin (local Hermes)
items.why: Vanta's 7 providers incl OpenRouter already aggregate models. Duplicate.

items.feature: browser_providers — pluggable browser backends (local Hermes)
items.why: Vanta has Playwright browser tools. Power-tooling, low ND priority.

items.feature: Full hooks plugin framework (lifecycle shell hooks)
items.why: Config surface + plugin discovery; keep ONLY the notify-on-finish idea (folded into proactive nudges), drop the framework.

items.feature: OpenTelemetry trace emitter plugin
items.why: Observability/enterprise; no end-user ND value.

items.feature: Batch trajectory generation / compression (training data)
items.why: Research/training tooling, not an end-user feature.

issues.note: GitHub issue triage (live, 2026-06-04). ND-lens. Both trackers are dominated by dev/platform/provider concerns with near-zero ND framing — which validates the opportunity. Worth-adding items are mostly F6 reliability lessons, not big features.

hermes.title: 'Converse mode' — agent won't act until you say go
hermes.note: Community ask. Plan-first gate; top ND fit (F4/F6). → PULL.

hermes.title: 'Can't type well, voice is huge' (accessibility)
hermes.note: Direct accessibility demand (F1/F3); supports voice-mode PULL.

hermes.title: Google Tasks integration
hermes.note: Task capture = F2/F5; pairs with Vanta's todo + gmail/calendar.

hermes.title: Setup wizard loops on stale credential pool entry
hermes.note: F6 — Vanta's setup must avoid this onboarding failure mode (initiation friction if onboarding breaks).

hermes.title: Temporary Gemini rate-limits surfaced as 'quota exhausted'
hermes.note: F6 — honest, accurate error mapping (Vanta manifesto: report only what's true).

hermes.title: 73% of every API call is fixed overhead
hermes.note: Token-overhead profiling; informs whether Vanta ever needs tool_search (not at 33 tools).

hermes.title: CJK/Chinese IME input + Chinese desktop UI
hermes.note: i18n breadth; Vanta is English/macOS-first single-user.

goose.title: Read context limit from meta.n_ctx for local servers
goose.note: F6 — reliable auto-compaction for local Ollama/llama.cpp; ties to the per-model capability matrix PULL.

goose.title: SKILL.md relative paths don't work — CWD is workspace not skill dir
goose.note: F6 — Vanta's skill execution should set CWD to the skill dir; cheap correctness fix.

goose.title: Register MCP extensions dynamically at runtime
goose.note: Vanta already has mount_mcp (live runtime mount, kernel-gated).

goose.title: Image paths containing spaces silently not detected
goose.note: Bug-class lesson: handle spaces in paths; not a feature.

goose.title: Azure Bearer auth / Bedrock GPT / Bedrock streaming

localHermesDelta.note: Customizations in ~/.hermes/hermes-agent vs the clean baseline — what Jason+Claude changed. (stream_* are now upstreamed; ai-gateway + browser_providers remain local-only.)

items.what: Typed frozen-dataclass event vocabulary (MessageChunk/Stop, Commentary, ToolCallChunk/Finished, LongToolHint, GatewayNotice) — names WHAT happened, not HOW delivered.
items.why: Clean agent→surface contract; Vanta's TUI feed would benefit from typed events vs ad-hoc callbacks. F3 calm output.

items.what: Per-surface router — an adapter can return None to 'eat' tool chrome on surfaces that can't render it; tool lines join one progress queue (kills draft-vs-tool race).
items.why: 'Eat noisy chrome per surface' IS ND output-regulation (F3). Port the design, not the Python.

items.what: An AI-gateway model-provider plugin (local-only; not in upstream).
items.why: Vanta's 7 providers + OpenRouter cover this.

items.what: Pluggable browser backends (local-only; not in upstream).
items.why: Vanta has Playwright browser tools.

items.what: Telegram send-draft formatting + cronjob tool adjustments.
items.why: Surface polish; revisit only if Vanta's telegram/cron output is noisy (F3).

claudeCode.note: Claude Code CLI survey (leaked-claude-code = partial leak/plumbing layer; claude-code-haha = runnability layer). Study-only; reimplement clean.

items.note: → PULL. Real Vanta gap: free-typed models 400 on unsupported params.
Decouples Ask-resolution from the kernel gate; ports cleanly. Vanta has no hooks layer.

items.finding: Hooks race the UI prompt (first wins) via stream-json control protocol
items.note: Programmatic policy auto-decides without blocking the user — could reduce approval fatigue. (Pull the idea, not the whole hooks framework.)

items.finding: Recovery/degraded readline CLI behind a flag

items.note: Gateway event-delivery robustness — verify vs Vanta's gateway first.
Optional native deps should degrade, never break the input loop.
ANTI-FINDING — do not lift the entrypoint to Node/tsx without stubbing.

items.finding: Not in leak: system prompt, compaction, todo UX, grep/glob/multiedit, thinking, prompt-caching
items.note: Partial leak — cannot source these. Honest gap.

docsTakeaways.hermes: 262 documented user stories. Primary personas: Developers/Technical (65), Personal Assistants (44), Business Ops (16), Content Creators (11), Researchers (9). CRUCIALLY, Hermes DOES have an accessibility/ND thread — but as edge cases, not the spine: voice-first interface ('can't type well, voice is huge' — motor accessibility), ADHD morning/evening standup workflows, proactive unprompted reminders (self-initiation support), memory persistence framed as cognitive-load reduction, and an NVDA screen-reader addon built by a blind user. Tagline 'an agent that grows with you' gestures at democratization beyond developers. TAKEAWAY: the ND/exec-function demand is real and latent in Hermes's own userbase — Hermes treats it as edge, Vanta's wedge is to make it THE spine. Validates the nudges / voice / memory / clarify PULLs.
docsTakeaways.goose: Guides cover purely technical infrastructure: sessions, context engineering, recipes, tools, projects, CLI, providers, config, MCP servers, logging, file management, task execution. Explicitly developer-centric ('enhanced code editing', 'codebase analysis', 'terminal integration'); assumes CLI/YAML/API fluency. Secondary enterprise/institutional framing (custom distributions, extension allowlist, sandbox). ZERO accessibility/neurodivergence/executive-function/everyday-assistance framing. Overlap with Vanta's ND vision: near-zero — correct and expected. Goose is the contrast case that sharpens Vanta's positioning.
